fix: remove lexical from peerDependencies — bundled as dependency

package they never use.

- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/plugins
- Update install instructions in all READMEs and docs

Users now only need:

commit.message:
Agent: Project Organization Sweep (#7494)

* Agent: Project Organization Sweep

* Initial plan

* Move annotate-static-overrides from refactor workspace to lint workspace

The transform `annotateStaticFunctionOverridesTransform` previously lived
in `src/refactor/src/annotate-static-overrides.ts` but had no connection
to the refactor domain (rename/restructuring transactions). Its sole
purpose is to set `_overridesStaticFunction = true` on AST nodes whose
corresponding property is exclusively read by the lint workspace's
`synthetic-comments.ts` to emit `@override` doc-comment tags.

Because the lint workspace never imports from `@gml-modules/refactor`, the
transform was effectively orphaned: defined but never called, making the
`_overridesStaticFunction` branch in `synthetic-comments.ts` permanently
dead.

New location: `src/lint/src/rules/gml/transforms/comments/annotate-static-overrides.ts`
Old location: `src/refactor/src/annotate-static-overrides.ts`

Changes:
- Moved the file to the lint `transforms/comments/` directory, co-located
  with `synthetic-comments.ts` which consumes its output
- Removed the lone `as any` cast; uses `MutableGameMakerAstNode & { id?: unknown }`
- Exported from `comments/index.ts` and `transforms/index.ts` barrels
- Registered as the first entry in `TRANSFORM_REGISTRY_ENTRIES` with an
  ordering comment explaining why it must precede `stripCommentsTransform`
- Added 9 unit tests in `src/lint/test/annotate-static-overrides.test.ts`
  using a proper `AnnotatedNode` type rather than `as any` assertions

Co-authored-by: henrylkirk <15679826+henrylkirk@users.noreply.github.com>

* Reduce cognitive complexity in collectConstructorInfos by extracting helpers

Extract resolveConstructorName, resolveParentConstructorName, and
collectStaticFunctions so collectConstructorInfos stays below the
sonarjs/cognitive-complexity threshold of 15.

Co-authored-by: henrylkirk <15679826+henrylkirk@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Agent: Target State Alignment (#7492)

* Agent: Target State Alignment

* Initial plan

* format: remove printBooleanReturnIf — enforce formatter/linter architectural boundary

The formatter was performing a semantic content rewrite in printBooleanReturnIf:
detecting `if (cond) { return true; } else { return false; }` and transforming
it to `return cond;` (and the negated variant to `return !cond;`).

This violates target-state.md §2.2 / §3.2: "Format must not perform
semantic/content rewrites — those belong in the lint workspace."

The transformation is already owned by @gml-modules/lint's
gml/optimize-logical-flow rule (condensation.ts:resolveSimpleBooleanReturnArgument
and traversal-normalization.ts:simplifyIfStatement), so duplicating it in the
formatter was a direct violation of the two-surface ownership model.

Changes:
- Remove printBooleanReturnIf, getBooleanReturnBranch,
  getBooleanReturnStatementInfo, negateExpressionDoc, needsParensForNegation
  from print.ts; IfStatement now calls buildIfStatementDoc directly
- Remove unused constants (TERNARY_EXPRESSION, LOGICAL_EXPRESSION,
  BINARY_EXPRESSION) that were only referenced by the removed functions
- Update test-flow.output.gml fixtures to show full if/else bodies
- Add two contract tests in formatter-boundaries-ownership.test.ts documenting
  that boolean-return simplification is lint-only behavior

Co-authored-by: henrylkirk <15679826+henrylkirk@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(python): add timeout parameter to generated RPC methods (#681)

* fix(python): add timeout parameter to generated RPC methods

Every generated async RPC method now accepts an optional `timeout`
keyword argument that is forwarded to `JsonRpcClient.request()`.
This lets callers override the default 30s timeout for long-running
RPCs like `session.fleet.start` without bypassing the typed API.

Fixes #539

* test: cover no-params and server-scoped RPC timeout branches

Add tests for PlanApi.read (session, no params) and ModelsApi.list
(server, no params) to exercise all four codegen branches.

* fix: move _timeout_kwargs after quicktype imports, add server+params test

- Move _timeout_kwargs helper after the quicktype-generated import block
  to avoid duplicate Optional import and keep preamble conventional
- Add ToolsApi.list tests covering the server-scoped + params branch
- All four codegen branches now have test coverage

* style: fix ruff format in test_rpc_timeout.py

* fix: use float | None instead of Optional[float] in generated RPC

Optional is not imported in the generated rpc.py file. On Python 3.11
(used in CI), annotations are eagerly evaluated, so Optional[float]
would cause NameError at import time. Use the modern float | None
union syntax which requires no import and matches the rest of the
generated code.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: wrap long docstring line in client.py to pass ruff E501

Pre-existing ruff line-length violation in get_last_session_id docstring
example that was failing CI on main.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: resolve pre-existing CI failures in docs and ruff lint

- docs/guides/custom-agents.md: use typed enum constants instead of
  string literals for PermissionRequestResultKind (C# and Go examples)
- python/copilot/client.py: wrap long docstring line to satisfy E501

Both issues pre-exist on main and block all PR CI runs.

commit.message:
perf(kernels): eliminate per-head/per-block allocations in CPU attention (#3289)

## Summary

Eliminates heap allocations in four hot-path CPU attention functions by
reusing buffers.

## Changes

### 1. \ lash_attention_cpu()\  stack-allocated block scores
- **Before**: \Vec::with_capacity(block_k)\ allocated per Qblock
iteration (inner loop)
- **After**: \[f32; BLOCK_SIZE]\ (32 elements, 128 bytes) on the stack,
reused across all iterations
- **Eliminated**: O(seq_q  ceil(seq_k/32)) heap allocations per call

### 2. \multi_head_attention()\  buffer reuse via \xtract_head_into()\
- **Before**: 3 \xtract_head()\ per head (allocates \Vec<f32>\ each
time)
- **After**: 3 buffers allocated once before loop, reused via
\xtract_head_into()\
- **Eliminated**: 3  num_heads allocations per call

### 3. \grouped_query_attention()\  same pattern
- **Before**: k/v extracted per kv_head, q extracted per q_head (all
allocating)
- **After**: 3 buffers allocated once, reused across both loops
- **Eliminated**: (2  num_kv_heads + num_q_heads) allocations per call

### 4. \ttention_forward()\ (ALiBi path)  same pattern
- **Before**: 3 \xtract_head()\ per head in ALiBi loop
- **After**: 3 buffers allocated once, reused via \xtract_head_into()\
- **Eliminated**: 3  num_heads allocations per call

## Impact

- **No behavioral change**  all functions produce identical output
- Reduces allocator pressure on the CPU attention path
- The existing \xtract_head_into()\ function was already implemented
but not used in these paths

## Testing

- Relies on CI (existing attention tests cover all modified functions)
- No new tests needed  this is a pure refactor with no behavioral change

## Triage: A (MERGE NOW)

Pure perf improvement, no behavior change, single file.

---------

[doc] add steps for checking out AutoPR branch locally (#37450)

Adds a new section "Steps to checkout to the `AutoPR`" in
`documentation/steps-after-generations.md` to guide users on how to
checkout an AutoPR branch locally.

AutoPR branches are created by a bot in the `azure-sdk/azure-sdk-for-js`
fork, which is different from `Azure/azure-sdk-for-js`. Users cannot
directly checkout or push to these branches via `origin`, so this
section provides two step-by-step options:

- **Option 1 (recommended):** Fetch a specific branch and create a local
tracking branch, which allows pushing changes back to the remote.
- **Option 2:** Fetch all branches from the remote and checkout, useful
when access to multiple branches is needed.

commit.message:
perf(kernels): eliminate Vec allocation in softmax_with_mask (#3292)

## Summary

Eliminates per-call heap allocations in two softmax functions.

## Changes

### 1. \softmax_with_mask()\  eliminate \Vec<f32>\ allocation
- **Before**: Allocated a \Vec<f32>\ (vocab_size 4 bytes, ~200KB) via
\.collect()\ to create a masked copy
- **After**: Writes masked values directly into output buffer, then
\softmax_f32_inplace()\

### 2. \softmax_topk()\  eliminate \Vec<bool>\ mask allocation  
- **Before**: Allocated \Vec<bool>\ (vocab_size bytes, ~200KB) as an
intermediate mask, then called \softmax_with_mask\
- **After**: Fills output with \NEG_INFINITY\, copies top-k values into
their positions, then \softmax_f32_inplace()\ directly

### Combined impact
- Eliminates ~400KB of per-token heap allocation during top-k sampling
- \softmax_topk\ saves one function call indirection

## Verification
- \ustfmt --check\ passes
- Functionally identical: same output values, same masked-position
zeroing
- 1 file changed across 2 commits

- **What changed**: \crates/bitnet-kernels/src/cpu/softmax.rs\ lines
313-375
- **Local verification**: \ustfmt --check\ (no build cache available)

Product mode (modes/product.md):
- DISCOVER→DESIGN handoff check: require customer docs or flag gap
- Requirement language standard: MUST/SHOULD/MAY with vague→measurable examples
- Spec template: traceable user stories, measurable metrics with targets
- Mandatory self-review pass: skeptical eng lead reviewer stance
- 8-item anti-pattern checklist (vague reqs, unmeasurable metrics, assumption-as-req,
  hand-waving, gold plating, missing personas, orphaned refs, contradictions)
- Severity classification: Critical (block) / Important (fix or note) / Suggestion (ignore)
- Max 2 review iterations before escalating to user
- TBD/TODO/PLACEHOLDER scan blocks STATUS: complete
- Structured escalation format: Context→Question→Options→Recommendation
- REPORT quality telemetry: TBD count, anti-pattern pass/fail, severity counts
- VALIDATE: falsifiable hypotheses, measurable success/failure criteria

Coordinator (SKILL.md):

fix: escape Spectre markup in /model selector to prevent silent cancellation (#123)

The ModelPicker.Pick UseConverter output contained unescaped provider names
in square brackets (e.g. [Claude Code]) which Spectre.Console interpreted
as invalid markup style tags. This caused an InvalidOperationException that
was silently caught, making /model always return 'Model selection cancelled.'

Fix: use [[...]] for literal brackets and Markup.Escape() for provider name,
matching the pattern already used in the startup ProviderOrchestrator selector.
Also wrap provider name in [dim] styling for visual consistency.

commit.message:
fix: resolve ServerAddress to NodeId in maintenance task sync (#8508)

* fix: maintenance task topology lookup, retry, and stale task cleanup

1. Strip gRPC port from ServerAddress in SyncTask using ToHttpAddress()
   so task targets match topology disk keys (NodeId format).

2. Skip capacity check when topology has no disks yet (startup race
   where tasks are loaded from persistence before first topology update).

3. Don't retry permanent errors like "volume not found" - these will
   never succeed on retry.

4. Cancel all pending tasks for each task type before re-detection,
   ensuring stale proposals from previous cycles are cleaned up.
   This prevents stale tasks from blocking new detection and from
   repeatedly failing.
